I did not understand the announcement.
Did you pack this bag yourself?
Did you declare everything?
Did you pack this bag yourself? Yes, I did.
Did you bring any liquids? No, I did not.
I am sorry, I did not understand. Could you repeat that, please?
What does that mean?
I did not catch that. Could you say it more slowly, please?
Once you know the vocabulary, security is a piece of cake.
Good morning. Passport and boarding pass, please. Did you pack this bag yourself?
Good morning. Yes, I did. I packed it yesterday, at home.
Thank you. Did you bring any liquids in your carry-on? Water, perfume, cream?
No, I did not. I know the liquid restriction, so I left my water at home.
Perfect. Now put your bag in the tray, and your phone and your keys too.
I am sorry, I did not understand. Could you repeat that, please? What does tray mean?
Of course. A tray is this box, here. Put your bag inside, and it goes through the X-ray machine. Then you walk through the metal detector.
Thank you. I understand now. And where is the departure lounge, please?
After the duty-free, on your right. Your gate is 22. Have a good flight, Mrs. Oliveira.
Yes, I did. I packed this bag myself.
No, I did not bring any liquids.
I have nothing to declare.
Excuse me, where is the departure lounge?

Good morning, and welcome to the security check. Please listen to these instructions before you arrive at the front of the line. First, the rule about liquids. You can only take small bottles: one hundred milliliters or less, and they all go inside one small clear bag. Water, perfume and cream are liquids. If your bottle is bigger than one hundred milliliters, you cannot take it, so please leave it here, in the box on your left. Second, the tray. Take out your laptop, your phone and your keys, and put them in the tray. Your coat and your belt go in the tray too. Then your bag and your tray go through the X-ray machine, and you walk through the metal detector. After the metal detector, take all your things from the tray, and please check that you have your passport and your boarding pass. Then you are free to go to the departure lounge. The duty-free is on your right. Thank you very much, and have a good flight.

Hello. My name is Teresa, and I am waiting for the Lisbon flight, like you. What a morning I had! At the security check, the officer stopped me. Do you know why? I did not remember my perfume. It was a big bottle, one hundred and fifty milliliters, and it was in my carry-on. So it was too big for the liquid restriction, and I lost it. I did not cry, but I was not happy. Then the officer said something very fast, about my coat and the tray, and I did not understand one word. I did not guess, and I did not say yes. I said: I am sorry, I did not catch that. Could you say it more slowly, please? And he did. He repeated it slowly, and he was very kind about it. You know, I did not feel bad. I am sixty-five years old, and I ask people to repeat all the time. Everybody does. And then, after security, I went to the duty-free and I bought another perfume. Why? Because after the security check there is no liquid restriction on what you buy there. You can take it on the plane. So I lost one perfume and I bought a better one. And you? Did you have a good morning?
